title: Free Designer Pattern: McQueen Dress
materials: fine wool suiting from north of England
short_description: SHOWstudio’s latest Design Download is an Alexander McQueen dress, the opening look in Sarah Burton’s Spring 2020 collection for McQueen. The dress is a reimagining of the Autumn/Winter 2000 Eshu dress, originally crafted in calico with a focus on the silhouette.
pattern_info: Pattern download comes in A4 sheets, with a test line to check the scale. Prints on 220 A4 sheets. Size: Sample size (model Akuac Thiep is wearing the sample size; she is 5’10” and wears a size 8/36; measurements: 34″ bust | 25″ waist | 35″ hip). Notions: Back zipper.
raw_text:
SHOWstudio’s latest Design Download is an Alexander McQueen dress.
A current-season design, it was the opening look in Sarah Burton’s Spring 2020 collection for McQueen.
This romantic collection drew comparisons with couture, featuring reworked old patterns and past-season fabrics, as well as Irish linens, damask or beetled, fine wool suiting from the north of England, and hand embroidery worked by the entire McQueen studio.
The dress re-envisions its show-opening counterpart in Alexander McQueen’s Eshu, named for the Yoruba trickster god and presented 20 years ago in a disused Hitchcock studio. As SHOWstudio notes, Burton’s “articulated puff-sleeve dress [is] a reimagining of the Autumn/Winter 2000 Eshu dress, originally crafted in calico with a focus on the silhouette.”
Burton’s dress makes repeated appearances in the Spring 2020 campaign.
The pattern download comes in A4 sheets, with a test line to check the scale.
Size: Sample size. Update: Model Akuac Thiep is wearing the sample size. She is 5’10” and wears a size 8/36. (34″ bust | 25″ waist | 35″ hip)
Notes: Prints on 220 A4 sheets.
Notions: Back zipper.
